content: read reviews/533.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/533.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/533.md.

It then notes: "Equivalently, the conjecture asks whether δ₃(5) = 0 ... This was disproved by Balogh and Lenz [BaLe13]."

# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist — `deepmind/533.lean`

Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
without an explicit justification.

---

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
  - Lines 1–15 match the required block verbatim.
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line).
  - Line 17: `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
  - Lines 19–25.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 533`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source.
  - `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/533](https://www.erdosproblems.com/533)`
- [ ] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.
  - **Missing.** The module docstring contains only the heading, reference link, and bibliography entry `[BaLe13]`. The plain-English description of the problem appears in the *theorem* docstring (lines 31–38) instead. The checklist requires it in the module docstring.
- [x] Optional: bibliography entries for related papers.
  - One entry: `[BaLe13]` (Balogh–Lenz).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside a namespace (`namespace Erdos533 ... end Erdos533`).
  - Lines 29 and 54.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos533`).
- [x] `open` statements appear **before** the namespace block.
  - Line 27: `open SimpleGraph` precedes `namespace Erdos533` on line 29.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- N/A — no custom `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations in this file.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Benchmark problems use the `theorem` keyword.
  - Line 41: `theorem erdos_533`.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_533` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions: `snake_case` for terms of Props.
- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` attributes on the **same attribute line**.
  - Line 40: `@[category research solved, AMS 5]`.
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
  - Lines 31–38.
- [x] Theorem docstring references source papers.
  - References `[BaLe13]`.

### `answer()` usage

The docstring states: "Let δ > 0. If n is sufficiently large and G is a graph on n vertices with no K₅ and at least δn² edges, then G contains a set of ≫_δ n vertices containing no triangle." It then notes: "Equivalently, the conjecture asks whether δ₃(5) = 0 ... This was disproved by Balogh and Lenz [BaLe13]."

The phrase "asks whether" indicates a **yes/no question**, so **Case 1** applies.

- [x] The theorem type is `answer(False) ↔ P`.
  - The conjecture was disproved by Balogh and Lenz [BaLe13], so `answer(False)` is correct.
- [x] The RHS (`P`) formalizes the **question as originally asked**, so that `answer(True)` would mean "yes" and `answer(False)` means "no".
  - P = for all δ > 0, there exists c > 0 and N such that for all sufficiently large n, every K₅-free graph G on n vertices with at least δn² edges contains a triangle-free set of at least cn vertices. Since the answer is no, `answer(False) ↔ P` correctly encodes `¬P`.
- [x] Quantifiers go **after** `answer(False)`, not as theorem parameters.
  - All quantifiers (`∀ δ`, `∃ c`, `∃ N`, `∀ n`, `∀ G`, `∃ S`) are on the RHS of `↔`.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Every `theorem` has **exactly one** `@[category ...]`.
- [x] The value is valid: `research solved`.
  - Appropriate since the conjecture was disproved by Balogh and Lenz [BaLe13] but no formal proof is linked.
- [x] Not marked `research open` (so no conflict with the sorry-free linter check).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Every `theorem` has **exactly one** `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number is listed: `5`.
- [x] Numbers are in **ascending numeric order** (single number — trivially satisfied).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Number is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (5 = Combinatorics).

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Proof uses `by sorry` (multi-line form: `:= by\n  sorry`).
  - Lines 51–52. Acceptable per checklist — multi-line proofs with `sorry` are fine when the theorem statement spans multiple lines.
- [x] No `sorry` appears in `def` or `abbrev` declarations.
  - N/A — no definitions in this file.
- [x] N/A — file is not under `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/`.
- [x] No informal proof sketches included.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A — no variants present. The file contains a single main theorem `erdos_533`.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable (`∀`, `∃`, `∈`, `≤`, `∧`, `↔`, `→`, `ℝ`, `ℕ`).
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` completes with **zero errors**.
  - Not verified — requires running the build.
- [ ] All five custom linters pass.
  - Not verified — requires running the build.

---

## Summary

**Result: PASS with one issue** (pending build verification)

### Issue found

1. **§3 — Module docstring missing plain-English description.** The module docstring (lines 19–25) contains the heading, reference link, and bibliography but omits a brief description of the problem. The description currently lives in the theorem docstring (lines 31–38). Move or copy a summary into the module docstring to satisfy checklist §3.

### Everything else

The file is otherwise clean. Copyright header, import, namespace, theorem naming, `answer(False)` usage (correctly encoding the disproved conjecture as Case 1), category/AMS attributes, proof body, and code quality all conform to the checklist. Build verification remains to be confirmed.

     2→Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.
     3→
     4→Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
     5→you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
     6→You may obtain a copy of the License at
     7→

     9→
    10→Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
    11→distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    12→WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    13→See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14→limitations under the License.
    15→-/
    16→
    17→import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18→
    19→/-!
    20→# Erdős Problem 533
    21→
    22→*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/533](https://www.erdosproblems.com/533)
    23→
    24→[BaLe13] Balogh, J. and Lenz, J., _Some exact Ramsey-Turán numbers_, 2013.
    25→-/
    26→
    27→open SimpleGraph
    28→
    29→namespace Erdos533
    30→
    31→/--
    32→Let $\delta > 0$. If $n$ is sufficiently large and $G$ is a graph on $n$ vertices with no $K_5$
    33→and at least $\delta n^2$ edges, then $G$ contains a set of $\gg_\delta n$ vertices containing no
    34→triangle.
    35→
    36→Equivalently, the conjecture asks whether $\delta_3(5) = 0$, where $\delta_3(5)$ is the
    37→Ramsey-Turán density. This was disproved by Balogh and Lenz [BaLe13], who
    38→showed $\delta_3(5) > 0$. The correct value is $\delta_3(5) = 1/12$.
    39→-/
    40→@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    41→theorem erdos_533 : answer(False) ↔
    42→    ∀ δ : ℝ, 0 < δ →
    43→      ∃ c : ℝ, 0 < c ∧
    44→        ∃ N : ℕ,
    45→          ∀ n : ℕ, N ≤ n →
    46→            ∀ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),
    47→              G.CliqueFree 5 →
    48→              δ * (n : ℝ) ^ 2 ≤ (G.edgeSet.ncard : ℝ) →
    49→                ∃ S : Finset (Fin n),
    50→                  c * (n : ℝ) ≤ (S.card : ℝ) ∧
    51→                  (G.induce (S : Set (Fin n))).CliqueFree 3 := by
    52→  sorry
    53→
    54→end Erdos533
    55→

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erdős Problem 533

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/533](https://www.erdosproblems.com/533)

[BaLe13] Balogh, J. and Lenz, J., _Some exact Ramsey-Turán numbers_, 2013.
-/

open SimpleGraph

namespace Erdos533

/--
Let $\delta > 0$. If $n$ is sufficiently large and $G$ is a graph on $n$ vertices with no $K_5$
and at least $\delta n^2$ edges, then $G$ contains a set of $\gg_\delta n$ vertices containing no
triangle.

Equivalently, the conjecture asks whether $\delta_3(5) = 0$, where $\delta_3(5)$ is the
Ramsey-Turán density. This was disproved by Balogh and Lenz [BaLe13], who
showed $\delta_3(5) > 0$. The correct value is $\delta_3(5) = 1/12$.
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_533 : answer(False) ↔
    ∀ δ : ℝ, 0 < δ →
      ∃ c : ℝ, 0 < c ∧
        ∃ N : ℕ,
          ∀ n : ℕ, N ≤ n →
            ∀ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),
              G.CliqueFree 5 →
              δ * (n : ℝ) ^ 2 ≤ (G.edgeSet.ncard : ℝ) →
                ∃ S : Finset (Fin n),
                  c * (n : ℝ) ≤ (S.card : ℝ) ∧
                  (G.induce (S : Set (Fin n))).CliqueFree 3 := by
  sorry

end Erdos533

     2→
     3→Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked

     5→

     8→`FormalConjectures/Util/`.
     9→
    10→---
    11→
    12→## 1. Copyright Header
    13→
    14→- [ ] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line):
    15→  ```
    16→  /-
    17→  Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.
    18→
    19→  Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
    20→  you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
    21→  You may obtain a copy of the License at
    22→

    24→

    26→  distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    27→  WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    28→  See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    29→  limitations under the License.
    30→  -/
    31→  ```
    32→- [ ] Year is exactly `2026` (the linter accepts any 4-digit year, but new files must use the current year).
    33→
    34→## 2. Import Statement
    35→
    36→- [ ] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line):
    37→  ```
    38→  import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    39→  ```
    40→- [ ] No other imports are present.
    41→
    42→## 3. Module Docstring
    43→
    44→- [ ] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
    45→- [ ] Starts with a heading: `# Erdos Problem N` (or appropriate title).
    46→- [ ] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source, e.g.:
    47→  ```
    48→  *Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/N](https://www.erdosproblems.com/N)
    49→  ```
    50→- [ ] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.
    51→- [ ] Optional: bibliography entries for related papers.
    52→
    53→## 4. Namespace
    54→
    55→- [ ] All declarations are inside a namespace (`namespace ErdosN ... end ErdosN`).
    56→  - Linter `linter.style.namespace` enforces this; error: *"The declaration '...' is not inside a namespace."*
    57→- [ ] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (e.g., `Erdos42`, not `erdos_42`).
    58→- [ ] `open` / `open scoped` statements appear **before** the namespace block when opening Mathlib namespaces (e.g., `open Filter Nat Real`).
    59→
    60→## 5. Custom Definitions
    61→
    62→- [ ] Helper `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations have a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
    63→- [ ] Definitions that involve classical choice or nonconstructive operations are marked `noncomputable`.
    64→- [ ] No `sorry` in definitions (only in theorem proof bodies).
    65→- [ ] Types and Props use `UpperCamelCase` (e.g., `IsCoveringSystem`).
    66→- [ ] Functions returning types use `lowerCamelCase` (e.g., `normalizedPrimeGap`).
    67→- [ ] Use `local notation` for problem-specific notation, scoped inside the namespace.
    68→- [ ] Consider adding `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for new definitions to verify they behave as expected.
    69→
    70→## 6. Theorem Statements
    71→
    72→- [ ] Benchmark problems use the `theorem` or `lemma` keyword (not `def` or `example`).
    73→- [ ] Main theorem is named `erdos_N` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
    74→- [ ] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions: `snake_case` for terms of Props.
    75→- [ ] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` attributes on the **same attribute line** (e.g., `@[category research open, AMS 11]`).
    76→- [ ] Main theorems and non-obvious variants have a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
    77→- [ ] Theorem docstrings should ideally reference the source paper(s) (e.g., `[Er95]`, `[AhKh97]`).
    78→
    79→### `answer()` usage

file.content:
# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist

Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
without an explicit justification.

Sources: [`formal-conjectures/README.md`](../formal-conjectures/README.md) (Style Guidelines §1–7),
[`AGENTS.md`](../formal-conjectures/AGENTS.md), and the five custom linters in
`FormalConjectures/Util/`.

---

## 1. Copyright Header

- [ ] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line):
  ```
  /-
  Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

  Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
  you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
  You may obtain a copy of the License at

      https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

  Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
  distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
  WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
  See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
  limitations under the License.
  -/
  ```
- [ ] Year is exactly `2026` (the linter accepts any 4-digit year, but new files must use the current year).

## 2. Import Statement

- [ ] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line):
  ```
  import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
  ```
- [ ] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [ ] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [ ] Starts with a heading: `# Erdos Problem N` (or appropriate title).
- [ ] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source, e.g.:
  ```
  *Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/N](https://www.erdosproblems.com/N)
  ```
- [ ] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.
- [ ] Optional: bibliography entries for related papers.

## 4. Namespace

- [ ] All declarations are inside a namespace (`namespace ErdosN ... end ErdosN`).
  - Linter `linter.style.namespace` enforces this; error: *"The declaration '...' is not inside a namespace."*
- [ ] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (e.g., `Erdos42`, not `erdos_42`).
- [ ] `open` / `open scoped` statements appear **before** the namespace block when opening Mathlib namespaces (e.g., `open Filter Nat Real`).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [ ] Helper `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations have a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [ ] Definitions that involve classical choice or nonconstructive operations are marked `noncomputable`.
- [ ] No `sorry` in definitions (only in theorem proof bodies).
- [ ] Types and Props use `UpperCamelCase` (e.g., `IsCoveringSystem`).
- [ ] Functions returning types use `lowerCamelCase` (e.g., `normalizedPrimeGap`).
- [ ] Use `local notation` for problem-specific notation, scoped inside the namespace.
- [ ] Consider adding `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for new definitions to verify they behave as expected.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [ ] Benchmark problems use the `theorem` or `lemma` keyword (not `def` or `example`).
- [ ] Main theorem is named `erdos_N` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
- [ ] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions: `snake_case` for terms of Props.
- [ ] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` attributes on the **same attribute line** (e.g., `@[category research open, AMS 11]`).
- [ ] Main theorems and non-obvious variants have a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [ ] Theorem docstrings should ideally reference the source paper(s) (e.g., `[Er95]`, `[AhKh97]`).

### `answer()` usage

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erdős Problem 533

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/533](https://www.erdosproblems.com/533)

[BaLe13] Balogh, J. and Lenz, J., _Some exact Ramsey-Turán numbers_, 2013.
-/

open SimpleGraph

namespace Erdos533

/--
Let $\delta > 0$. If $n$ is sufficiently large and $G$ is a graph on $n$ vertices with no $K_5$
and at least $\delta n^2$ edges, then $G$ contains a set of $\gg_\delta n$ vertices containing no
triangle.

Equivalently, the conjecture asks whether $\delta_3(5) = 0$, where $\delta_3(5)$ is the
Ramsey-Turán density. This was disproved by Balogh and Lenz [BaLe13], who
showed $\delta_3(5) > 0$. The correct value is $\delta_3(5) = 1/12$.
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_533 : answer(False) ↔
    ∀ δ : ℝ, 0 < δ →
      ∃ c : ℝ, 0 < c ∧
        ∃ N : ℕ,
          ∀ n : ℕ, N ≤ n →
            ∀ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),
              G.CliqueFree 5 →
              δ * (n : ℝ) ^ 2 ≤ (G.edgeSet.ncard : ℝ) →
                ∃ S : Finset (Fin n),
                  c * (n : ℝ) ≤ (S.card : ℝ) ∧
                  (G.induce (S : Set (Fin n))).CliqueFree 3 := by
  sorry

end Erdos533

lines: +Let $\delta > 0$. If $n$ is sufficiently large and $G$ is a graph on $n$ vertices with no $K_5$
+and at least $\delta n^2$ edges, must $G$ contain a set of $\gg_\delta n$ vertices containing no
+triangle? Equivalently, is $\delta_3(5) = 0$? Disproved by Balogh and Lenz [BaLe13].
[BaLe13] Balogh, J. and Lenz, J., _Some exact Ramsey-Turán numbers_, 2013.
